text.title: GitLab CI/CD Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Jobs MUST declare `needs:` to form a DAG; sequential `stages:` without `needs:` are rejected when ≥3 jobs (wastes pipeline minutes).
rule.rationale: DAG unlocks parallelism that stage-only pipelines cannot achieve; minutes saved compound.

rule.statement: Sensitive variables (credentials, tokens) MUST be `protected: true` AND `masked: true`. Plain unmasked variables in CI/CD settings are rejected.
rule.rationale: Unmasked variables leak in logs; unprotected variables run on every branch and amplify leak surface.

rule.statement: Conditional execution MUST use `rules:` (modern, expressive). `only:` / `except:` are legacy and rejected for new pipelines.
rule.rationale: Only/except cannot express composite conditions; rules+if covers all cases.

rule.statement: Pipelines MUST include SAST + secret detection at minimum; dependency scanning when third-party deps exist; DAST for HTTP services.
rule.rationale: Without baked-in scanners, security work falls outside dev loop and accumulates as debt.

rule.statement: Deployment jobs MUST set `environment:` with `name`, `url`, and `deployment_tier`. Untyped environments are rejected.
rule.rationale: Untyped environments break the deployment dashboard; tiers (production/staging/testing) drive policy.

rule.statement: Skip when repo not on GitLab OR single-job pipeline OR no DevSecOps requirement.
rule.rationale: GitLab features don't translate to other platforms; below complexity-threshold methodology is overhead.
